Off Calendar May-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SHELBY BONS AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Appearances (by Zoom):
Tamany Vinsonbentz, Esq. 310-991-0456 California IP Law for Plaintiff.
Padmini Cheruvu, Esq. 510-451-3300 Donahue Fitzgerald for Defendant.
Court reporter: Angela Sinclair, CSR #13902, 209-601-8366, angelatsinclair@gmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

Argued.
Ms. Cheravu contests and presents arguments; reconsider ruling based on 3 premises; take another look at evidence in Opposition; problematic, not bailiff, no personal knowledge, French CCP article 656, have declaration, context of situation; service at Father's house; service not reasonably calculated; specific jurisdiction.
Court comments and poses questions; expert on French civil procedure.
Ms. Vinsonbentz responds; declaration establishes French attorney, reasonableness of bailiff's acts, specific jurisdiction, cites case authority, rise to personal jurisdicion.
Ms. Cheruvu's brief rebuttal; doc from bailiff not sufficient, hearsay, evidence not before court re receiving trade secret.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Shelby Bons's motion to quash service of summons.

Taken Under Submission.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Ms. Cheruvu argues and presents arguments; alleged personal service on proof of service, request to adopt TR.
Ms. Culp contests and presents arguments; defect on POS of Defendant Bons, lynchpin of the TR, personal service, followed French law, Hague Convention and state law, cites exhibit G.
Court comments and poses questions, not about proof of service but equity of this default.
Ms. Chevaru responds to Plaintiff's arguments; direct service is broad.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

1 - Defendant Shelby Bons' Motion to Set Aside Default/Default Judgment/Leave to Defend.

Taken Under Submission.***(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Elizabth Magana, CSR #13533, 661-361-0296, emaganacsr@yahoo.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Aurora Solar Inc.'s unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Douglas E. Etts Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Douglas E. Etts has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) Counsel declares he "ha[s] not filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in California Superior Court." Counsel's testimony begs the question whether he has applied to appear PHV in the state appellate courts or any other court in this state? The clear intent of the requirement is to assure the court counsel is not seeking to appear PHV with such frequency that they should seek admission to the State Bar rather than PHV status. On this record the court cannot make the necessary determination.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
